ab: Ilumaq a qaudas-binanau’az?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Uu, ihaan nai palan masabaq.
en: Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

ab: Ilumaq a qaudas-binanau’az?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Uka, laupang nai munata, na maqmutin mulumaq.
ch: B:不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
en: No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

ab: Ilumaq a qaudas-bananaz?
en: Is grandfather at home?

ab: Ihaan nai ni-kai, munda’za’a ta kilim siata.
ch: B:他在二樓，你上去找他吧。
en: He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.

ab: Haiza bunun ilumaq?
en: Is anyone at home?

ab: Ai! Taupas qana, Talmadin nitu pasaduu, masihal aupa?
ch: B:是Taupas啊！很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
en: It’s Taupas. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

ab: Masihal! kilim saak suu pababazbaz.
en: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

ab: Isaq suu tama i?
en: Where is your father?

ab: Ihaan nai pipit’ia’an ta makal’ing sanglav, u’na’un zaku.
ch: B:他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
en: He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.

ab: Isaq a Ibu?
en: Where did Ibu go?

ab: Munhaan nai patulkukan ta madamu tulkuk.
en: She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

ab: Qodas, sumin saam.
en: Grandfather, we’re here.

ab: Uka muu qodas ilumaq, ihaan nai quma ta, ihaan a lumaq tun mantatala.
ch: B:你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他回來吧。
en: Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.

ab: Tama-Ibi! Sumin qana kasu.
en: Uncle, you’re here!

ab: Talmadin nitu pasaduu, muslu’in asu taldaqvas i.
en: I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.

ab: Tuza tu manahip saduu suu mun’iti.
en: It’s so good to see you.

ab: Pimuu a bunglai’un, pukumbu’i haan piskakazha’van ta.
ch: B:這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
en: Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

ab: Talmadin asu nitu tantungu a, na luklasuk naingka amin muskun maun.
ch: A:這麼久沒來，我叫大家一起來吃飯。
en: It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

ab: Azoq azaq, na muaz saaq quu ucia, haan ata bukzavan ta pababazbaz.
ch: B:不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
en: No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.

ab: Uninang suu, na mulumaqin saak.
ch: A:謝謝你的招待，我要回去了。
en: Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

ab: Haiza laupang kinulut tu tulkuk’un, dasi mulumaq, kasasaus’i maun.
ch: B:這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
en: This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.
